prose: Frontier-orbitals of pyridine computed with chemkit's frontier-orbitals skill via PySCF at DFT B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier 'standard'), exact four-center integrals (density fitting off by default), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). Geometry: user-supplied pyridine.xyz (11 atoms), used as-is at the fixed input geometry (single point, no relaxation). SCF converged in 10 cycles; engine integrity status 'ok', trustworthy = true. Headline HOMO-LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV. HOMO (index 20) = -7.2525992 eV; LUMO (index 21) = -1.112786 eV; HOMO-1 = -7.4731156 eV; HOMO-2 = -8.0425195 eV; LUMO+1 = -0.67231536 eV; LUMO+2 = 1.1781069 eV. Total electronic energy = -248.3782 Hartree (-6758.7152 eV). Koopmans-style descriptors: vertical IP = 7.2525992 eV, vertical EA = 1.112786 eV, electronegativity = 4.1826926 eV, chemical hardness = 3.0699066 eV, electrophilicity index = 2.8494218 eV (orbital estimates, not an excitation energy). No warnings were reported in the result JSON (clean run). The run used --accept-defaults to consent to the gas-phase default (no --solvent); the functional, basis, and tier were all specified explicitly as requested. cli_invocation: chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 --accept-defaults .../pyridine.xyz.
